title: Farmers
description: Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. Manage public and private forested lands for economic, recreational, and conservation purposes. May inventory the type, amount, and location of standing timber, appraise the timber's worth, negotiate the purchase, and draw up contracts for procurement. May determine how to conserve wildlife habitats, creek beds, water quality, and soil stability, and how best to comply with environmental regulations. May devise plans for planting and growing new trees, monitor trees for healthy growth, and determine optimal harvesting schedules. Work with agricultural scientists in plant, fiber, and animal research, or assist with animal breeding and nutrition. Set up or maintain laboratory equipment and collect samples from crops or animals. Prepare specimens or record data to assist scientists in biology or related life science experiments. Conduct tests and experiments to improve yield and quality of crops or to increase the resistance of plants and animals to disease or insects. (Based on 16 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Cover areas to specified depths with pesticides, applying knowledge of weather conditions, droplet sizes, elevation-to-distance ratios, and obstructions; spray or dust chemical solutions, powders, or gases into rooms, onto clothing, furnishings, or wood, or over marshlands, ditches, or catch basins; direct or assist other workers in treatment or extermination processes to eliminate or control rodents, insects, or weeds.

Measure, test, or weigh items to ensure they meet specifications; maintain records of testing results or other documents as required by governing agencies; monitor and control temperature of products; maintain or repair agricultural facilities, equipment, or tools to ensure operational readiness, safety, and cleanliness; perform regular maintenance of laboratory equipment by inspecting, calibrating, cleaning, or sterilizing.

Conduct studies of nitrogen or alternative fertilizer application methods, quantities, or timing to ensure satisfaction of crop needs and minimization of leaching, runoff, or denitrification; record environmental data from field samples of soil, air, water, or pests to monitor the effectiveness of integrated pest management (IPM) practices; promote precision agriculture information to reduce negative environmental impacts of farming practices and participate in efforts to advance precision agriculture technology, including developing advanced weed identification.

Collect information regarding soil and field attributes, yield data, or field boundaries, using data recorders and GIS; analyze remote sensing imagery to identify relationships between soil quality, crop canopy densities, light reflectance, and weather history; identify areas needing pesticide treatment by analyzing geospatial data to determine insect movement and damage patterns.

Conduct agricultural research, analyze data, prepare reports, summarize field productivity or profitability, and create maps showing precision agricultural data, such as crop yields, soil characteristics, and input applications.

Assist farmers in upgrading equipment, providing advice on feed and health maintenance of livestock, growing practices, and financial planning; establish short- and long-term plans for the management of forest lands and resources; assist with the conservation of wildlife habitats and water quality; advise on the development or application of better boom-spray technology.

Conduct classes or deliver lectures on various subjects like nutrition, home management, and farming techniques; organize, advise, and participate in community activities such as county and state fair events and 4-H Clubs; prepare and distribute educational materials for informational purposes.

Prepare land for cultivated crops, orchards, or vineyards by plowing, discing, leveling, or contouring; perform crop production duties such as tilling, hoeing, pruning, weeding, or harvesting crops; transplant trees, vegetables, or horticultural plants; plant grass with seed spreaders, and operate straw blowers to cover seeded areas.

Examine animals or crop specimens to determine the presence of diseases or other problems and collect animal or crop samples; treat minor injuries and ailments and contact veterinarians for serious illnesses or injuries; observe animals to detect symptoms or approach of estrus; record or compile test results or prepare graphs, charts, or reports.

Supervise agricultural technicians or farm laborers as well as activities related to pest or weed control operations; schedule and make regular visits to farmers; maintain records of services provided and evaluate the effects of advice given.

Plan and supervise forestry projects, including planting trees, managing tree nurseries, thinning forests, and monitoring growth; negotiate contracts for forest harvesting and management; map forest area soils and vegetation to estimate standing timber and future value; plan conservation projects for wildlife habitats and water quality.
Provide advice and recommendations as a consultant on forestry issues; collaborate with producers to diagnose and prevent management and production problems.

Monitor contract compliance and results of forestry activities to ensure adherence to regulations; examine forests or forest nurseries and identify areas in need of treatment or pest control; evaluate the effects of forestry operations on wildlife populations and habitats.
Analyze tests of seeds to evaluate seed viability, germination rates, and to determine appropriate treatment and prevention methods for pest problems.
Determine methods of cutting and removing timber while minimizing waste and environmental damage; analyze the effects of forest conditions on tree growth rates and species prevalence.

Contact local forest owners to gain permission for inventorying standing timber; monitor wildlife populations and assess impacts of forest operations; establish collaborations for pest control or agricultural education; set and monitor production targets.

Conduct public educational programs on forest care and conservation; communicate with social service and health professionals to advise families on management practices; supervise other food science technicians.
Remove blockages to facilitate pest control and provide drainage.

Assure environmental regulations compliance and conservation practices in all forestry and farming activities; Define the roles and responsibilities of agricultural scientists in plant, fiber, and animal research as well as animal nutrition.
Prepare or incubate slides with cell cultures; perform laboratory testing and specialized techniques to monitor health and growth of crops and livestock.
Provide direct assistance to farmers by performing purchasing or selling products and supervising properties; manage their financial obligations concerning services provided.
Conduct insect or plant disease surveys; research information requested by farmers; prepare data summaries or analyses of agricultural findings.
Assess comparative soil erosion from various planting or tillage systems to determine effective practices for crop management.
Not relevant.

Direct or assist workers engaged in the maintenance or repair of agricultural equipment, such as power tools or motorized machinery, and monitor or oversee construction projects like irrigation systems; instruct and train workers in tasks such as transplanting, pruning trees or shrubs, and maintaining equipment. Inspect machinery operation to detect malfunctions, adjust, repair, and service farm machinery as necessary.

Operate or tend various equipment used in agricultural production, such as tractors, combines, seed drills, and irrigation machines, and drive trucks to haul crops, supplies, tools, or farm workers. Load and unload crops or materials manually or using equipment, and perform mixing or application of fertilizers, insecticides, or herbicides to enhance crop yield.

Plant or maintain vegetation through activities such as mulching, fertilizing, watering, mowing, or pruning. Identify diseases or pests affecting crops or landscapes and order appropriate treatments, and monitor project activities to ensure that deadlines and schedules are maintained.

Prepare or maintain required records, such as work activity reports or inventory of supplies, and perform administrative duties related to personnel, such as hiring workers or processing time sheets. Monitor financial reports to manage budgets for maintenance or development of agricultural operations.

Confers with managers or landscape architects to develop plans for landscaping maintenance or improvements, and analyze conditions of crops, fields, or plant stock to determine cultivation, spraying, weeding, or harvesting needs. Tour grounds, parks, gardens, or forests to inspect conditions of plants and soil.

Estimate costs for projects such as landscaping, groundskeeping, or agricultural production, prepare service estimates based on labor and material costs, and negotiate with customers about fees and services. Maintain budgets for individual projects and ensure that sufficient supplies are available.

Schedule work for crews based on priorities, availability, or weather conditions, and coordinate work activities with other supervisors or departments to ensure efficient workflow and compliance with safety standards. Prepare and keep records of personnel actions related to performance evaluations, hires, or promotions.

Manage both public and private forested lands for economic, recreational, and conservation purposes; communicate with forestry personnel regarding forest harvesting or management plans. Determine methods of facilitating harvesting or logistics for timber and other resources.

Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops, and study the effects of alternative practices on soil health and crop productivity. Conduct tests or experiments to improve yield and quality of crops or resilience against pests and diseases.

Negotiate pricing and contracts for the procurement of supplies and equipment, and confer with other departments to optimize resource utilization in agricultural operations. Issue equipment to workers and oversee the procurement of necessary materials and supplies.

Assess and advise on environmental practices that contribute to wildlife habitat conservation and compliance with regulations on land use and agricultural practices. Consider sustainability factors in agricultural practices and operations to enhance productivity while minimizing environmental impact.

Observe animals for signs of illness, injury, or unusual behavior, and notify veterinarians or managers as warranted. Treat animal illnesses or injuries, following experience or instructions of veterinarians. Assign tasks such as feeding and treatment of animals, and cleaning and maintenance of animal quarters. Train workers in techniques such as planting, harvesting, weeding, or insect identification and in the use of safety measures. Monitor workers to ensure that safety regulations are followed, warning or disciplining those who violate safety regulations. Record the numbers and types of fish or shellfish reared, harvested, released, sold, and shipped.

Conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on characteristics such as yield, quality, disease resistance, nutritional value, or adaptation to specific soils or climates. Conduct research to determine best methods of planting, spraying, cultivating, harvesting, storing, processing, or transporting horticultural products. Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests. Study soil characteristics to classify soils on the basis of factors such as geographic location, landscape position, or soil properties. Develop methods of conserving or managing soil that can be applied by farmers or forestry companies. Investigate responses of soils to specific management practices to determine the use capabilities of soils and the effects of alternative practices on soil productivity. Identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics. Conduct experiments investigating how soil forms, changes, or interacts with land-based ecosystems or living organisms. Perform chemical analyses of the microorganism content of soils to determine microbial reactions or chemical mineralogical relationships to plant growth.

Conduct experiments to investigate the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment. Develop environmentally safe methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests. Study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, such as the use of new methods of composting. Research technical requirements or environmental impacts of urban green spaces, such as green roof installations. Conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels or in the production of green fuels.

Survey undisturbed or disturbed lands for classification, inventory, mapping, environmental impact assessments, environmental protection planning, conservation planning, or reclamation planning. Consult with engineers or other technical personnel working on construction projects about the effects of soil problems and possible solutions to these problems. Provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation. Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects. Communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public or teach related courses, seminars, or workshops.
Train workers in tree felling or bucking, operation of tractors or loading machines, yarding or loading techniques, or safety regulations.

emerging_tasks.task: Establish driving routes for pesticide applications.
Operate drone technology to capture aerial imagery and data for crop monitoring and analysis.
Record information about pesticide applications, such as the type used and amount applied.
Use new technology and equipment, such as drones or GPS systems, to apply pesticides more accurately and efficiently.
Operate drones to monitor crop health, growth and pest infestations, and apply targeted treatments.
